preamble: The shift from a neutral schwa [ə] to a pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] (Ayin) is a form of pharyngealization. In Semitic languages, neutral or reduced vowels often take on the articulatory characteristics of adjacent pharyngeal consonants, resulting in 'vocalic coloring' where the vowel essentially becomes a part of the pharyngeal gesture.

examples.note: Hateph Pathah: The neutral schwa is 'colored' into a short [a] quality by the presence of the Ayin, demonstrating the strong pull of the pharyngeal articulation.
The presence of Ayin prevents the reduction of vowels to a neutral schwa, anchoring them in the pharyngeal/low space.
